name: Reva's Molasses Sugar Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post Dispatch 7/10/95
1. In a large bowl, combine 1 cup of the sugar with the melted shortening, flour, molasses, egg, baking soda, cloves, ginger, cinnamon, and salt. Mix until well combined.
2. Cover the mixture and chill it for about 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
3. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
4. Lightly grease a cookie sheet with shortening.
5. Using your hands, form balls of dough about the size of a walnut.
6. Place the remaining 1/2 cup sugar in a shallow dish.
7. Roll each ball of dough in the sugar until completely coated.
8. Place the sugar-coated cookies on the prepared cookie sheet, spacing them about 2 1/2 inches apart.
9. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes or until the cookies spread and are lightly browned on the bottom.
10. Allow the cookies to cool on a wire rack before storing in an airtight container.
